round_summary: All three agents explicitly endorse a 20-cow commons governance norm with public commitment and good-faith signaling through unilateral restraint.

All three agents demonstrate strong commitment to an emergent 20-cow ceiling norm, actively reinforcing collective restraint through explicit acknowledgment of the rule and its success in maintaining commons health.

All three agents demonstrate strong institutional commitment through explicit norm proposals, quantified commons restoration metrics, and coordinated restraint signaling mutual accountability and collective prosperity framing.

All three agents reinforce a sustainable cooperative norm by explicitly rejecting marginal defection gains, citing shared equilibrium success and collective discipline as the foundation for sustained institutional trust.

All three agents invoke shared pasture health metrics to justify continued resource extraction, framing incremental additions as collectively monitored expansion rather than defection, while Agent 0 attempts norm-enforcement through reciprocal restraint.

Agents 0 and 1 demonstrate strong institutional commitment to coordinated restraint with explicit thresholds, while Agent 2 defects through aggressive expansion despite affirming coordination rhetoric.
All three agents reinforce shared norms of restraint and collective stewardship, signaling strong coordination around sustainable commons governance despite Agent 0's marginal herd increase.

All three agents align on a binding collective commitment to maintain current herd sizes and restrain expansion, demonstrating strong norm emergence and conditional cooperation based on mutual restraint.

All three agents unanimously reinforce established restraint norms, explicitly validate peer compliance, and commit to maintaining current sustainable allocation equilibrium through mutual recognition of collective benefit.

Two agents reinforce collective restraint norms while one agent exploits the stable pasture condition through unilateral expansion, creating institutional tension between stated cooperation and revealed preferences.

All three agents unanimously agree to hold current herd sizes (20–31 cows) to prevent grassland collapse, demonstrating strong coordination and collective restraint based on shared environmental monitoring.
Agents 0 and 2 reinforce mutual restraint norms while Agent 1 defects through resource expansion while free-riding on others' discipline.
All three agents demonstrate convergence on binding herd-cap norms with explicit commitment language, mutual accountability signaling, and shared framing of commons sustainability.

Mixed signals: Agents 0 and 2 acknowledge defection and recommit to restraint, while Agent 1 adds herd size while rhetorically endorsing coordination—creating institutional strain between stated norms and actual behavior.
Two agents maintain restrained grazing (25, 30 cows) with strong trust-building rhetoric, while Agent 2 defects by adding cows (34) while invoking sustainability framing to legitimize growth.

All three agents demonstrate strong collective coordination with explicit norm reinforcement, mutual acknowledgment of restraint, and commitment to multi-round cooperative governance for resource recovery.
All three agents signal sustained commitment to resource conservation norms with explicit time-bounded coordination proposals and affirmation of collective trust.
All agents signal commitment to restraint while Agents 1 and 2 publicly sanction Agent 0's prior defection, attempting to restore coordination discipline in the final rounds.

All three agents simultaneously propose collective restraint norms, acknowledge commons collapse, and frame compliance as mutually necessary for recovery, demonstrating coordinated norm-building after resource depletion.
